4-pin reflective IR sensor on the FRONT CASTER wheel; reads a black/white CHECKERED pattern on the wheel = rotation/odometry encoder (distance travelled / motion confirmation). Feeds U10 (LM293/393 comparator) -> STM32. Trace J20 pins to U10 and the toggling output to an STM32 pin
J20 pin1 = GND (board side)
J20 pin3 = GND (board side). Sensor is a 2-diode package (emitter + receiver) in one body; pins 1 & 3 = the two cathodes/grounds

J20 pin2 = RECEIVER = odometry SIGNAL -> resistor divider -> series R -> U10:2 (LM393/293 IN1-, inverting input). U10:3 (IN1+) = reference set by a divider WITH positive feedback FROM U10:1 (OUT1) => SCHMITT TRIGGER (hysteresis) -> clean de-bounced odometry square wave. U10:1 (OUT1) = squared pulse -> STM32 PD2 [CONFIRMED] = the MCU odometry pulse input (PD2 = EXTI2; count edges for caster distance). J20 fully mapped

J20 caster-wheel odometry: U10 ch1 Schmitt output -> STM32 PD2. PD2=EXTI2; caster_odo.c counts both edges (caster_odo). VERIFIED counting 2026-06-25. HARDWARE QUIRK: it is a SWIVEL caster - the encoder disc only aligns with the sensor when the caster TRAILS straight back (~+/-20deg), i.e. only while the robot drives roughly STRAIGHT FORWARD. During turns / when the caster is swivelled, the disc leaves the sensor and the count FREEZES. => 'odo' = forward-travel distance only, NOT a turn/heading sensor. Good for: forward-distance + anti-stall (wheels turn but odo frozen = slipping/stuck). Emitter J20:4 + U10 need SWITCHED_SENSOR_5V (PE5)
J20 pin4 = EMITTER IR LED, driven from the 5V sensor rail (SWITCHED_SENSOR_5V) through a 3.9 ohm resistor (marking 3R90). Illuminates the black/white caster disc

U10 = LM293/393 DUAL comparator, pin8=VCC(SWITCHED_SENSOR_5V), pin4=GND. CH1 (1/2/3) = J20 caster ODOMETRY Schmitt trigger (IN1-=pin2 from J20:2 divider, IN1+=pin3 ref w/ feedback from OUT1, OUT1=pin1 squared pulse) -> OUT1 -> STM32 PD2 [CONFIRMED]. CH2 (5/6/7) = CURRENT/OVERCURRENT detect across R100 (=0.100 ohm shunt) on Q25's load. TODO: OUT2(pin7)->? (overcurrent flag to STM32 or protection)

J17:5 -> STM32 PC0 (= ADC123_IN10, analog sense). RESOLVED: J17:5 + GND = the side IR TRANSCEIVER's RECEIVER output. User clarified the 'unique side sensor' is a transceiver (priyomoperedatchik = emit+receive), which he had mis-called a 'receiver'. Its EMITTER is the FORWARD-facing LED, wired in SERIES with the cliff down-LED on the shared J17:2 drive (2 LEDs in series: one faces DOWN=cliff, one faces FORWARD=side); emitter-string anode = 5V via D3 -> J17:8. So pin5 = side-transceiver receiver out; emitter drive = pin2; this is SEPARATE from the 3-pin base/dock receiver on pins 6/8

J17:1 = SWITCHED 5V sensor-power rail (per user: this 5V turns on together with the sensors AND the wheel motors - a shared sensor/motor power domain). battery low-side current sense: shunt R72||R73 -> U4 A2 amp -> U4:7 (OUT2) -> R71 -> STM32 PA7 = ADC_IN7. Ring-out 2026-06-24. In firmware (ch7, mean). Cal: I[mA]=(mV-1180)*1000/488 (4-pt fit 0.1-0.5A)

battery pack VOLTAGE sense -> STM32 PA2 = ADC_IN2 (per user). Resistive divider to the pin, SAME 71.5k/8.2k as VIN/PA1 (x9.72). Cal from flashed ADC 2026-06-24: 14.47 V pack = 1481 mV pin => V_pack = V_pin * 14470/1481 (x9.77). In firmware ch2 mean; console 'vbat' shows real pack volts. (Earlier 1839mV point was bogus - taken before firmware was flashed)

24V INPUT rail sense -> STM32 PA1 = ADC_IN1 (per user). External body-connector aux 24V input AND dock power, diode-OR'd onto one line; SAME node as dock J10 but through a diode (PA1 rail = J10 - 1 diode drop). DIVIDER: top 71.5k (mark 7152, to Vin) + bottom 8.2k (mark 8201, to GND) => V_rail = V_pin * 79.7/8.2 = V_pin*9.72. In firmware ch1 mean; console 'vin' shows real rail volts. 24V -> ~2469 mV at pin
